rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: source-side only; Ainu epic recitation background — no LLM mechanism overlap
HaluGate — token-level real-time runtime hallucination detection at generation time; directly overlaps runtime gate + interval-check + hallucination function; name 'gate' nearly identical
HaMI — adaptive token selection for hallucination detection; runtime token-interval mechanism for same purpose
broad survey; overlaps topic but not specific cadence mechanism
multi-layered hallucination mitigation framework — layered runtime gating for hallucination overlaps functional role
LLM-as-judge — different mechanism (judge model rather than cadence features)

stylometric watermarks — uses identical feature set (sentence-length distribution, function-word frequency, cadence features) on LLM output; the candidate's cadence-classifier features are essentially this prior art repurposed as a hallucination gate

fail_reason: Four independent hits. HaluGate (rank 2) is a near-identical runtime token-interval hallucination gate — the candidate's K-token interval gate mechanism is essentially the same architecture with cadence features substituted. HaMI (rank 3) covers adaptive token-level hallucination detection at runtime. Stylometric Watermarks (rank 8) already employs the exact feature set proposed (sentence-length distribution, function-word frequency, stylometric/cadence features) over LLM output — only the downstream use (watermark vs hallucination gate) differs. Multi-Layered Mitigation (rank 5) covers layered runtime gating. The 'prosody/cadence' framing does not establish novelty: the underlying mechanism (runtime interval check on output statistics with early-stop/rollback on deviation) is already present in the cited prior art, and the specific features are already enumerated in stylometric-watermark literature.
